topStories.headline: US and Iran Sign MOU to Reopen Strait of Hormuz; Wall Street Surges
topStories.summary: The US and Iran finalised a memorandum of understanding on June 14 to end 3.5 months of conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, ending the largest energy supply disruption in history.
topStories.marketImpact: S&P 500 ↑ 1.65%, Dow ↑ 0.92% to 51,671, Nasdaq ↑ 3.07%; Brent crude ↓ ~4% to ~$83–84/bbl; Boeing ↑ 4.66%, Nvidia ↑ 3.40%, Amazon ↑ 3.12%

topStories.headline: FOMC Meeting Day 1 of 2 — No Cut Expected, Hawkish Bias Shift On the Table
topStories.summary: The Fed begins its two-day June meeting with a 99.6% market-implied probability of no rate change; a shift in forward bias from easing to neutral or tightening is the key watch for Wednesday's statement.
topStories.marketImpact: Rate-sensitive sectors ↓ potential; USD ↑ on hawkish shift; any surprise tightening bias → equities ↓

topStories.headline: Bank of Japan Set for Historic Rate Hike to 1% — Without Governor Ueda
topStories.summary: The BOJ is expected to raise its benchmark rate 25bps to 1% — its highest since 1995 — at a meeting being held without Governor Ueda, who is hospitalised for a hepatic cyst infection.
topStories.marketImpact: JPY ↑ vs USD; Japanese financials ↑; Nikkei under potential pressure; yen carry trades ↓

topStories.headline: May CPI at 4.2% YoY as Iran War Energy Shock Bites — Energy Costs Up 23.5%
topStories.summary: The May Consumer Price Index rose 0.5% MoM and 4.2% YoY, driven by a 23.5% spike in energy costs linked to the Strait of Hormuz closure.
topStories.marketImpact: Keeps Fed firmly on hold or hawkish; bond yields elevated; energy-sensitive consumer names ↓ on margin pressure

topStories.headline: Nikkei Tops 69,000 for First Time; KOSPI Surges 5.2% on Iran Peace Deal
topStories.summary: Asian markets surged Monday as the US-Iran MOU removed a major oil-shock risk for import-dependent economies — Nikkei 225 hit 69,289 while South Korea's KOSPI reached 8,697.
topStories.marketImpact: SK Hynix ↑ 4%+, Samsung ↑, SoftBank ↑, Kioxia ↑; KRW and JPY strengthening; semiconductor supply chain watch

topStories.headline: China Economy Slows Sharply — Industrial Output at Near 3-Year Low
topStories.summary: China industrial production grew just 4.1% last month, its weakest in almost three years, while fixed-asset investment unexpectedly shrank 1.6% and manufacturing capacity utilisation fell to 73.9%.
topStories.marketImpact: Materials ↓; China-linked miners watch; stimulus expectations rising but structural reform constraints limit scope

companiesToWatch.name: Boeing
companiesToWatch.reason: Surged 4.66% Monday on Iran deal; reopening of Hormuz air corridors and geopolitical de-escalation are direct earnings tailwinds.

companiesToWatch.name: NVIDIA Corp.
companiesToWatch.reason: Up 3.40% as tech sentiment rebounds; Fed meeting outcome is the key near-term risk. AI infrastructure demand narrative still intact.

companiesToWatch.name: Amazon
companiesToWatch.reason: Up 3.12%; cloud AI buildout spending and broader risk-on sentiment driving recovery after recent volatility.

companiesToWatch.name: Exxon Mobil
companiesToWatch.reason: Brent crude fell ~4% on Hormuz MOU — direct revenue headwind for oil majors; watch closely as deal implementation progresses.

companiesToWatch.name: Jabil Inc.
companiesToWatch.reason: Serenity's recent call on its 1.6T transceiver business; AI photonics chokepoint thesis gaining traction in retail circles.

companiesToWatch.name: X-Fab Silicon Foundries
companiesToWatch.reason: Serenity re-affirmed his CPO/photonics thesis over the weekend; originally surged 70–77% on his May 27 post; watching for continuation.

latestPicks.thesis: Positioned as 'the next TSEM' at ~$1.4B market cap; competing for H2 2027 CPO scale-up inflection point; Serenity posted new weekend shower thoughts re-affirming the thesis.
Market has not priced Jabil's 1.6T LRO pluggable-transceiver business; ~40% rerating potential by H1 2027 as hyperscalers expand CPO deployments.
CW laser supplier for Jabil, Ayar, POET, and Marvell — framed as the chokepoint light source for CPO/Silicon Photonics; called 'the next $LITE'.

priorPickMoves.move: Jumped 70–77% in a single session on May 27 after Serenity's original post (confirmed by Bloomberg).
Up 165% in one week following Jabil partnership thesis and Serenity coverage.

serenity.sentiment: Highly positive and viral — 500K+ followers on X, featured by Lookonchain, Futunn, and Odaily. Self-reported 2026 YTD return of +4,502% (leveraged, unaudited). Some scepticism about unaudited figures, but retail enthusiasm remains very high.

quickHits: Nvidia raised its quarterly dividend significantly, signalling long-term AI revenue confidence (CNBC)
Goldman Sachs reset oil price forecast to $85/bbl average for 2026 — above pre-war levels even with Hormuz deal (AOL)
Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index due this week — a key inflation signal given 4.2% CPI print (Kiplinger)
China-Japan rare earth tensions escalating as Beijing tightens export controls amid US tariff pressure (Lowy Institute)
